title: Understanding Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: The rotational kinetic energy of a rotating object can be calculated using the formula: \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: For a uniform solid sphere, the moment of inertia \( I \) is given by \( I $$=$$ \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \). Use the given mass \( M $$=$$ 2*10**{30} \) kg and radius \( R $$=$$ $${10}^4$$ \) $$m$$ to calculate \( I \).

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: Substitute the values into the formula \( I $$=$$ \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \).

title: Find the Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity \( \omega \) can be found using the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2\pi}{T} \), where \( T \) is the period of rotation.

title: Calculate the Angular Velocity
text: Substitute the period \( T $$=$$ $$0.02$$ \) seconds into the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2\pi}{T} \).

title: Calculate the Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Now that you have \( I \) and \( \omega \), substitute them into the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \) to find the rotational kinetic energy.

title: Final Calculation
text: Substitute \( I $$=$$ 8*10**{37} \) and \( \omega $$=$$ 100\pi \) into \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \).
